t_ch: 唉，还是做一只自由自在的岁阳比较快乐。
t_en: "sigh" It's so much better being a free and roaming heliobus.

t_ch: 十分尴尬，一点都不严肃！
t_en: It's totally awkward and not serious at all!

t_ch: 原来如此，是我登场的时机不太严肃，让你尴尬了呀。真抱歉，人类。
t_en: I see, it's the timing of my entrance that isn't serious enough, and feels awkward. Sorry for that, human.

t_ch: 等下次不太严肃的场合时，我再突然冒出来。除非你是个「一直很严肃」的人。
t_en: I'll pick a time in the future when it's less serious to suddenly pop out, then. Unless you're one of those "always serious" types.

t_ch: 要学会快乐呀，人类。
t_en: You should learn how to be happy, human.

t_ch: 总之，下次别这么登场了！
t_en: Whatever — just don't make an appearance like that next time!

t_ch: 别人不喜欢，你该不会因此而放弃呼吸吧？
t_en: If someone else didn't like it, you wouldn't just stop breathing, would you?

t_ch: 如果要让说书的人下次别再讲别人说过的故事，那他或许会伤心难过、怀疑自己。
t_en: If you tell storytellers not to tell any stories that someone else has told before, they would feel hurt — or start to doubt themselves.

t_ch: ——可惜你问的是岁阳。
t_en: ...Pity you're only asking a heliobus.

t_ch: 我是梗小鬼，就算人类不喜欢，我也不会停下来的，嘿嘿。
t_en: I'm Memey the Imp, and I'm not gonna stop doing something just because a human doesn't like it. Teehee!

t_ch: 好好拿着我的礼物吧，拜拜！
t_en: You enjoy that gift, okay. Bye!

t_ch: 这是一个平平无奇的香炉。
t_en: This is just a normal incense burner.

t_ch: （将它往外面再拉一点）
t_en: (Pull it out a little more)

t_ch: 香炉就像是走在半道上不愿意再继续散步的大狗，使劲往外拉拽也无济于事。
t_en: The incense burner is like a large dog who refused to keep walking while splayed halfway down a path — pulling it out by force doesn't work in the slightest.

t_ch: …也许你可以考虑换个方向？
t_en: ...Maybe a change of direction would help?

t_ch: （将它往里面推一点）
t_en: (Push it in slightly)

t_ch: 虽说是平平无奇的香炉，它却如此突出。
t_en: It may be merely a normal incense burner, but in a way it's also outstanding.

t_ch: 就像是有一位香炉领导对着众香炉说「你们之中谁想接受这个困难的任务，就向前迈出一步」，结果其他香炉默默地往后退了一步。
t_en: It's like an incense burner leader barking at its incense burner rookies: "Whosoever wants to take on this difficult assignment, take one step forward!" — and seeing them all shuffle back in silence.

t_ch: 为了这小小香炉考虑，也为了让强迫症能好受一些，应该将它往里面再推一些。
t_en: For the sake of this wee little incense burner — and also to alleviate my intrusive thoughts — we should push it in a little more.

t_ch: （我什么都不会做的）
t_en: (I'm not doing anything)

t_ch: 你什么都不会做的，但是…你可以随时回来。
t_en: You're not doing anything, but... you can also come back any time you want.

t_ch: 嚯哈哈哈！我被你发现了！
t_en: O-hoho! You found me!

t_ch: 嗯？还以为是恚炎，真是吓了我一跳。
t_en: Oh, I thought you were Furynox... You spooked me for a second there.

t_ch: 啊，对了，我记得恚炎已经没了…被浮烟那个坏东西给吞掉了，那这个东西我留着也没什么用，你拿着吧。
t_en: Oh yes, that's right — Furynox isn't with us anymore... It got gobbled up by that wicked Cirrus. So, I won't be needing this any more. This is yours now.

t_ch: 只是因为我将香炉推进去了？
t_en: All because I pushed the incense burner in?

t_ch: 是啊，只有有心之人才会做这样的事。你得到了我的认可，所以我要给你奖励。
t_en: Yes, only a considerate person would ever do such a thing. You've earned my approval, so I want to reward you.

t_ch: 你们岁阳身上怎么带着大还丹？
t_en: Why is a heliobus packing a Grande Rejuvepill anyway?

t_ch: 怎么了吗？我要是说「岁阳身上有次元口袋」的话，你会感到奇怪吗？
t_en: What about it? Would you think it was strange if I told you heliobi have an extra-dimensional pocket inside our bodies?

t_ch: …岁阳的事，你可以不用这么清楚的。
t_en: ...You don't have to get all up in heliobi business.

t_ch: 恚炎，好熟悉的名字，是谁啊？
t_en: Furynox, that's a familiar name. Who's that again?

t_ch: 那是个愚蠢的家伙，早早地占据了绥园，却被浮烟给骗了。他们俩自称商量出了一个能绝对战胜十王司的计策。结果呢，笑死我了。
t_en: It's that knucklehead who used to haunt Fyxestroll Garden but got tricked by Cirrus. They claimed to have devised this strategy capable of decisively defeating the Ten-Lords Commission, but it just left me laughing my pants off.

t_ch: …不过，我还挺喜欢跟恚炎捉迷藏的，唉。
t_en: ...However, I quite like playing hide-and-seek with Furynox. *sigh*

t_ch: 很好，我收下了。
t_en: Great, I'll take it.

t_ch: 虽然有些臭臭的，但是很好用，拿去吧。
t_en: Here's something for you. It might be a little stinky, but it's also very useful. Take it.

t_ch: …那个，我正在思考别的奖励方式，你有没有什么好主意呢？
t_en: ...Well, I'm trying to think of other ways to reward you. Got any good ideas?

t_ch: 用香炉摆出一个圆形的圈…
t_en: Use the incense burner to form a circle...

t_ch: …再准备上一个缺口？妙啊！
t_en: ...and leave a gap? Genius!

t_ch: 不过和现在的做法相比，有些重复，让我再考虑一下。
t_en: But it's getting a bit repetitive now. Let me have a think.

t_ch: 在显眼的地方摆上一块大石头…
t_en: Leave a big boulder in an obvious place...

t_ch: …你搬起石头，我冒出来！妙啊！
t_en: ...and when you move the boulder, I APPEAR! Brilliant!

t_ch: 不过，我感觉大部分人都不会搬石头，我会一直躲着，不见天日。
t_en: But something tells me most people won't move the boulder, and I'll just be left hiding there in pitch blackness.

t_ch: 在空中放置些画着标靶的气球…
t_en: Float some balloons with targets on them high up into the sky...

t_ch: …你把所有的气球射下来，然后我再冒出来？
t_en: ...and when you shoot down all the balloons, I pop out?

t_ch: 万一你没有弓和箭该怎么办呢，我可买不起……
t_en: What if you don't have a bow and arrow? I don't have the cash for that...

t_ch: …你能不能直接给我奖励呢？
t_en: ...What if you just give me the reward straight?

t_ch: 我是没啥意见啦…但，你们人类生活中最重要的不就是仪式感吗？
t_en: I have no complaints... But isn't a sense of ceremony something on which you humans place great importance?

t_ch: 要庆祝出生的那一天，结识的那一天，还有完蛋的那一天。这些行为不都很多余吗？
t_en: Celebrating the day you were born, the day you met, and the day you died... Isn't it all just so tedious?

t_ch: 所以，能不能直接给你奖励呢？不行。你完成挑战，我给你奖励，这是仪式感。
t_en: So you ask, can I JUST give you the reward? No. Complete the challenge and I'll give you the reward: THAT is the rule.

t_ch: 算了，我自己再想想吧。
t_en: Whatever, I'm going to have another think.

t_ch: 好好拿着我的礼物吧，拜拜！
t_en: You enjoy that gift, okay. Bye!

t_ch: 唉……真伤脑筋……
t_en: Sigh... what a real headache...

t_ch: 我怎么就和朋友走散了呢……
t_en: How did I get separated from my friends...?

t_ch: 他大概会什么都不做，在原地等我。但我实在也懒得动弹……
t_en: He would probably do nothing and just sit there. But I'm really too lazy to move...

t_ch: 所以，我想，那个，有些难以启齿呢，该怎么说好呢？
t_en: So, I think, that um... it's a little hard to put into words. How should I say it?

t_ch: 我猜，你是想让我带你过去？
t_en: So, you want me to take you there?

t_ch: 是这样的，我只有依赖你了。
t_en: That's it, I'll count on you.

t_ch: 看我一棒把你打飞过去。
t_en: Watch me swat you away with my trusty bat.

t_ch: 请放下棒子说话好吗？咱们岁阳是不可能被物理手段击飞的唷。
t_en: Could you please put down the bat and talk? It's impossible for us heliobi to be harmed by physical means.

t_ch: 所以，人类能不能借你的身体载我一路。
t_en: So, human: Could you please lend me your body to carry me across.

t_ch: 有话请直说。
t_en: If you've something to say, say it.

t_ch: 请你把我带过去，可以吗？
t_en: Ferry me across, please?

t_ch: 形势不太妙，我准备撤退！
t_en: That sounds like trouble. Time to haul!

t_ch: 别别别，听我说完，我只是想让你带我过去而已……
t_en: Wait no, listen to me. I just want you to bring me over there...

t_ch: 很简单的，我是岁阳，你是人类。我可以附身于你，你可以走路，你走到我的朋友身边，我再解除附身。这件事情就结束了，我会给你小小的奖励。
t_en: It's simple: I'm a heliobus, and you're a human. I can latch onto you. Then, you walk over to my friend, I detach myself, and everything will be over. I will give you a small token of appreciation.

t_ch: 所以，我想附身于你，可以吗？
t_en: So then, I would like to attach myself to you. Do I have your permission?

t_ch: 这难道不是一件危险的事吗？
t_en: Isn't this dangerous?

t_ch: 你也太警惕了，十王司是不是给你讲了些什么……
t_en: You're too on edge. Did the Ten Lords tell you something...?

t_ch: 不要担心，我今天没有胃口的…当然，我知道这样的说法不能打消你的疑虑…我向燎原保证…不，我向燧皇保证，你不会有事的！
t_en: Don't worry about it. I have no appetite for today... Of course, I know just saying that won't dispel any suspicions... But I swear to Ignamar... No, I swear to the Flint Emperor, you'll be just fine!

t_ch: 你附身吧，我带你过去。
t_en: Come on then, and I'll carry you over.

t_ch: 太好了，这份恩情在找到我朋友之前，我是不会忘记的。
t_en: Great. I will never forget this show of kindness... until I get to my friends.

t_ch: 让我再考虑考虑……
t_en: Let me give it some thought...

t_ch: 你再考虑考虑吧………
t_en: Please consider...

t_ch: 你附身吧，我带你过去。
t_en: Come on then, and I'll carry you over.

t_ch: 太好了，这份恩情在找到我朋友之前，我是不会忘记的。
t_en: Great. I will never forget this show of kindness... until I get to my friends.

t_ch: 太好了，这份恩情在找到我朋友之前，我是不会忘记的。
t_en: Great. I will never forget this show of kindness... until I get to my friends.

t_ch: 让我再考虑考虑……
t_en: Let me give it some thought...

t_ch: 你再考虑考虑吧………
t_en: Please consider...

t_ch: 唉……真伤脑筋……
t_en: Sigh... what a real headache...

t_ch: 我怎么就和朋友走散了呢……
t_en: How did I get separated from my friends...?

t_ch: 他大概会什么都不做，在原地等我。但我实在也懒得动弹……
t_en: He would probably do nothing and just sit there. But I'm really too lazy to move...

t_ch: 你附身吧，我带你过去。
t_en: Come on then, and I'll carry you over.

t_ch: 太好了，这份恩情在找到我朋友之前，我是不会忘记的。
t_en: Great. I will never forget this show of kindness... until I get to my friends.

t_ch: 太好了，这份恩情在找到我朋友之前，我是不会忘记的。
t_en: Great. I will never forget this show of kindness... until I get to my friends.

t_ch: 让我再考虑考虑……
t_en: Let me give it some thought...

t_ch: 你再考虑考虑吧………
t_en: Please consider...

t_ch: 好想前往朋友身边啊……
t_en: I really want to go over to my friends...

t_ch: 追上了！
t_en: All caught up!

t_ch: 喂，你这家伙，又在随便溜人类啊！
t_en: Hey, are you making humans go on errands for you again?

t_ch: 绥园里实在太无聊啦，不然呢？
t_en: It's so boring in Fyxestroll Garden. What else could I do?

t_ch: 溜人类？
t_en: Making humans go on errands?

t_ch: 岁阳哪会像人类那样累的跑不动哪！
t_en: Heliobi wouldn't get tired to the point of not being able to travel like humans do!

t_ch: 这家伙只是随便差遣人类，真搞不懂这有什么好玩的。
t_en: This guy just sends humans on errands after errands! I really have no idea what the fun in that is.

t_ch: 这样真的开心吗？
t_en: Are you really happy like this?

t_ch: 不知道你开心了没有，我、我…我现在是挺开心的……
t_en: I don't know if you're happy or not, but I... I... I'm pretty happy...

t_ch: 这就结束了吗？以后还有吗？
t_en: Is this all? Will there be nothing else?

t_ch: 你要是喜欢的话，那就最好了。
t_en: If that's what you want, then it's for the best.

t_ch: 你要是不喜欢的话…嗯，我…我也不知道你喜欢不喜欢，我就假装没看见吧。
t_en: If that's not what you want... well, I... I don't know whether you want it or not, so let's pretend I didn't see it.

t_ch: 好好拿着我的礼物吧，拜拜！
t_en: You enjoy that gift, okay. Bye!

t_ch: 对了，你记得吗？我们已经见面<color=#dbc291ff>3次</color>了。
t_en: By the way, do you remember? We've met <color=#dbc291ff>3 times</color> now.

t_ch: 我原本还准备了<unbreak>900</unbreak>个谜题给你的，但是我有些累了……
t_en: I had over <unbreak>900</unbreak> puzzles prepared for you, but I'm getting tired...

t_ch: 我决定一次给你来个大发送，全部都给你了。
t_en: So I've decided to give you everything all at once.

t_ch: 这个好，都给我吧。
t_en: I'd like that. Gimme everything.

t_ch: 放心吧，我都准备好了。
t_en: Don't worry. I've got everything ready.

t_ch: 你的产能够做<unbreak>900</unbreak>个谜题吗？
t_en: Do you even have the capacity to do <unbreak>900</unbreak> puzzles?

t_ch: 我虽然没有本事，但是有这样的志向。
t_en: I may not have the capability, but I've got the aspiration.

t_ch: 好好拿着我的礼物吧，拜拜！
t_en: You enjoy that gift, okay. Bye!

t_ch: 嚯哈哈哈！我被你发现了…你是不是已经疲惫了？
t_en: Hohoho! You found me... Are you exhausted?

t_ch: …其实我疲惫了。
t_en: ...Actually, I'm exhausted.

t_ch: 我原本还准备了<unbreak>900</unbreak>个谜题给你的，但是我现在不想了……
t_en: I had over <unbreak>900</unbreak> puzzles prepared for you, but I don't feel like it anymore...

t_ch: 哟，伙计！不得不说，游戏确实有让人心情舒畅的魔力，我感觉自己越来越上手了！
t_en: Hey, buddy! I have to admit, gaming really does have a soothing magic to it. I feel like I'm getting better and better!

t_ch: 美梦排排乐玩过了吗？
t_en: Have you played the Dreamy Slots before?

t_ch: 还挺有意思的。虽然玩法简单，但一玩就停不下来——每次二缺一的时候，我身上就好像有真蛰虫在爬！
t_en: It's pretty interesting! It's a simple game, but once you start, you just can't stop! Every time I get two out of three, I feel like I'm actually covered in True Stings!

t_ch: 幸运转盘玩过了吗？
t_en: Have you played the Lucky Wheel before?

t_ch: 玩过了，还挺刺激的。看着指针跟大奖擦肩而过的时候，那落差别提有多大了！心里就像坐过山车一样！
t_en: I have. It's so exciting! The way my heart races and drops as the pointer goes past the grand prize, it's like a real roller-coaster ride!

t_ch: 黄金扭蛋机玩过了吗？
t_en: Have you played the Golden Capsule Machine before?

t_ch: 玩过了，还挺惊喜的。感觉它就像一台失控的自动购物机——但是非常巨大！
t_en: I have. I was so surprised! It feels like an out-of-control shopping machine... But huge!

t_ch: 看上去你挺乐在其中啊。
t_en: You seem to really enjoy it?

t_ch: 哈哈！原本我还以为，想要掌握娱乐的诀窍，必须像学知识一样下苦功夫才行……
t_en: Haha! I was under the impression that if you want to grasp the secrets of entertainment, you have to work and really study...

t_ch: 结果真正上手玩之后，才发现这些设施并不复杂，是我一直没过去心里的那道坎。
t_en: Eventually, I realized that once you start playing, they're really not that complicated. They're just a challenge I've never taken on!

t_ch: 多亏你的开导，我可以尽情享受匹诺康尼的乐趣了——求之不得啊！
t_en: Thanks to your guidance, I can enjoy Penacony to its fullest. What more could I ask for!

t_ch: 来瓶苏乐达庆祝下吧。
t_en: How about a SoulGlad to celebrate?

t_ch: 你让我想通了这么大一件事，应该我请你才对。
t_en: You really helped me think things through. I should be treating you.

t_ch: 拿着吧，它将见证我新生活的开始。
t_en: Take this. It's a symbol of the beginning of my new life.

t_ch: 有时间的话，就来<color=#dbc291ff>艾迪恩公园</color>找我吧。
t_en: If you have time, come and find me in <color=#dbc291ff>Aideen Park</color>.

t_ch: 自动留言：「欢迎尊贵的《狸狸周刊》社长{NICKNAME}回到{F#她}{M#他}忠实的杂志社，请选择您的日程计划——」
t_en: Automated Message: "Welcome back, esteemed president of Furbobo Weekly, {NICKNAME}, where your loyal editorial team awaits instructions. Please select your schedule."

t_ch: 进入「狸狸通信」。
t_en: Enter Furbobocom.

t_ch: 查看狸狸福利。
t_en: Check Furbobo perks.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 自动留言：「再见，祝社长大人拥有快乐、美好的一天哩！」
t_en: Automated Message: "Goodbye, and have a wonderful, happy day, Boss!"

t_ch: 更改八卦论坛的名字。
t_en: Change the name of the gossip forum.

t_ch: 自动留言：「社长大人觉得{TEXTJOIN#254}不好吗？那您想要给它起个什么名字呢？」
t_en: Automated message: "Boss, you don't like {TEXTJOIN#254}? What name would you prefer it to have, then?"

t_ch: 月Chat！
t_en: Moon Chat!

t_ch: 谒神月！
t_en: Death Notes!

t_ch: 乐园杂俎！
t_en: Gossip Grove!

t_ch: 自定义名称3
t_en: Custom Name 3

t_ch: 狸狸八卦
t_en: Furbobo Gossip

t_ch: 自动留言：「您确定吗？」
t_en: Automated message: "Are you sure?"

t_ch: 就这个了！
t_en: This is the one!

t_ch: 自动留言：「好的，您已更改八卦论坛名称为：{TEXTJOIN#254}」
t_en: Automated message: "Done! Forum name updated to: {TEXTJOIN#254}"

t_ch: 不，让我再想想。
t_en: No, let me think about it.

t_ch: 叮咚叮咚！现在是乐园历<unbreak>1999</unbreak>年的一个天气宜人的时刻，幻太子我注意到，浣熊大人已经坐在办公桌前，辛辛苦苦工作了大半天，一直都没有休息啵！
t_en: Ding-dong, ding-dong! Here we are in the Arcadian Calendar year <unbreak>1999</unbreak>. On this fine and pleasant day, I, Paraprince, have noticed that the Great Raccoon has been sitting at {F#her}{M#his} desk, keeping {F#her}{M#his} nose to the grindstone all day without a single break!

t_ch: 老板多工作，员工才能多发钱！
t_en: The harder the boss works, the more the staff get paid!

t_ch: 有这么自觉的老板，《狸狸周刊》的销量再也不用发愁了哔波！
t_en: With such a conscientious boss, Furbobo Weekly's sales won't be a problem, boop!

t_ch: 其实，我刚坐下来。
t_en: Actually, I just sat down.

t_ch: 噫！噫！是吗？浣熊屁拍错方向了……
t_en: Huh? Really? Guess I brown-nosed the wrong person...

t_ch: 可能是浣熊大人存在感太强了，让我感觉到您一直就在这里，太可靠太安心了。
t_en: Maybe it's because your presence is so strong, it feels like you've always been here. So reassuring. So comforting.

t_ch: 还有空看我？你没用心上班。
t_en: You've got time to watch me? Slacker.

t_ch: 哔哔哔不是的哔波！身为一名专业的记者，很难不职业病发作……
t_en: Beepbeep, that's not it! It's an occupational hazard of professional journalists...

t_ch: 我这双滴溜溜的大眼睛，就算一直盯着电脑屏幕，余光也一直在关注着身边的信息啵。
t_en: Even when my big, sparkling eyes are glued to the screen, my peripheral vision is always gathering intel.

t_ch: 无论如何，现在我要对浣熊大人说出「STOP！」了啵。请您停下手头的工作，仔细想一想，今天是不是有什么很重要很重要的事情遗漏了没做？
t_en: Anyway, I gotta say "STOP!" to the Great Raccoon right now! Please pause your work and think carefully: did you forget something super-duper important today?

t_ch: 出门忘带手机了？
t_en: Forgot my phone?

t_ch: 浣熊大人也有拿着手机找手机的时候，滤镜…要碎了啵……
t_en: Even the Great Raccoon looks for their phone... while holding it. There goes my rose-tinted glasses...

t_ch: 刷日常，领奖励？
t_en: Farm dailies, get rewards?

t_ch: 这也很重要…哎，你这个人啊，真是满脑子只想着奖励啵。
t_en: This is important, too... Tsk, you only have rewards in your brain, don't you?

t_ch: 不会是忘发工资了吧？
t_en: Don't tell me I forgot your salary?

t_ch: 猜错了！不过，虽然今天不是发薪日，但我不介意多一笔奖金啵！
t_en: Wrong! But hey, even though it's not payday, I wouldn't say no to a bonus!

t_ch: 一定要我说出那两个字吗？怎么会这样？看来…我只有把那两个字清晰地说出来，浣熊大人才能回想起来。
t_en: Do I really have to say that word? How did it come to this? Looks like... the only way the Great Raccoon will remember is if I spell it out crystal-clear.

t_ch: 到底是什么？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 快说快说，不要卖关子。
t_en: Come on, spill it! Don't leave me hanging.

t_ch: 为了浣熊大人的健康着想，我幻太子只好将我脆弱的脸皮，弃置于此……
t_en: For the sake of the Great Raccoon's health, I, Paraprince, have no choice but to cast aside my fragile pride...

t_ch: <size=28>您——今天还没有<color=#dbc291ff>提肛</color>啵！！！</size>
t_en: <size=28>Yooouuu haven't done your <color=#dbc291ff>kegels</color> today!!!</size>

t_ch: 请暂停下手头的事情，起来走走，喝点水，活动一下脖子，看看杂志社窗外的风景，并时刻注意：不要忘记每日提肛五十次哔啵！
t_en: Please pause what you're doing, get up and walk around, grab some water, stretch your neck, enjoy the view outside the office, and remember: fifty kegels a day!

t_ch: 社长大人，为了《狸狸周刊》能准时发稿，我的狸猫爪子，已经敲得快要磨平哩！
t_en: Boss! To get Furbobo Weekly out on time, I've been typing so hard my furbo paws are practically worn down to nubs!

t_ch: 可困意像克拉肯的墨汁一样喷涌而出，一片乌漆嘛黑的阴云覆盖了杂志社上方。
t_en: But a wave of drowsiness burst forth like kraken ink, shrouding the office in darkness.

t_ch: 社长大人能不能一棒子把我敲晕在办公桌前，一觉醒来，本狸的脸说不定已经自动滚出一篇报道哩……
t_en: Boss... could you knock me out at my desk? Maybe when I wake up, an article will have magically rolled right off my face...

t_ch: 考验社长经营之道的时候到了！为了鼓励手下的狸猫记者，你决定——
t_en: Time to put your executive skills to the test! To motivate your furbo journalists, you decide to...

t_ch: 使用激将法。
t_en: Use reverse psychology.

t_ch: 你贴在普狸策毛绒绒的小耳朵旁边，发出暧昧、邪恶而残忍的恶魔低语：「幻太子已经比你多写<unbreak>3000</unbreak>字了！」
t_en: You lean close to Furbulitzer's fluffy little ear and whisper in a low, wicked, devilish tone: "Paraprince has already written <unbreak>3,000</unbreak> more words than you!"

t_ch: 什、什么？好心机的幻太子，明明和我说它只写了<unbreak>1000</unbreak>字，这个叛狸。
t_en: Wh—What!? That scheming Paraprince! It clearly told me it'd only written <unbreak>1,000</unbreak>! Traitor furbo.

t_ch: 再见了社长大人，我要回去火速赶稿！今晚的杂志社，将被我拆家般的键盘声彻底轰炸哩！
t_en: Farewell, Boss! I'm heading back to crank out a manuscript at warp speed! Tonight, I'll deafen the newsroom with the sound of my keyboard!

t_ch: 我给大家点咖啡！加大剂量！
t_en: Coffee's on me, everyone! Extra shot!

t_ch: 咿——呀！还有这种好事？我马上在咱们工作群里@所有人哩！
t_en: Whoa, no way! This deal's too good to miss! I'm going to ping everyone in the work chat right now!

t_ch: 没出息地吊死在杂志社门口。
t_en: Pathetically hang yourself at the magazine entrance.

t_ch: 那能怎么办呢？你是一个没出息的社长，只能想出最窝囊的办法：在死线来临之前，亲自吊死在杂志社门口谢罪。
t_en: So what can you do? You're a pathetic excuse for a boss who can only come up with the most cowardly solution: hang yourself outside the magazine office as an apology before the deadline hits.

t_ch: 噢不，不可以！好不容易请来了新社长，《狸狸周刊》不能再次倒闭哩！
t_en: No, absolutely not! We finally got ourselves a new president. Furbobo Weekly can't go under again!

t_ch: 为了杂志社的未来，我会忍辱负重…负重前行…眼皮好重…我好像看见太爷爷哩…对不起太爷爷，我还不能过去，我要去写稿……
t_en: For the future of the magazine... I'll endure... press on... my eyelids are so heavy... I think I see great-grandpa... Sorry, great-grandpa, I can't join you yet. I still have articles to write...

t_ch: 你也别太拼了…给工作群的大家说一下，我请大家喝咖啡。
t_en: Don't push yourself too hard... Tell the work chat I'm buying everyone coffee.

t_ch: 社长大人！咖啡到哩！社长大人快来尝尝吧。
t_en: Boss! Coffee's here! Drink it while it's hot!

t_ch: 咖啡店的「轻脚宅急送」以鸽川冲浪般的速度送到了，发稿前难得的休憩时刻，你精心品尝了普狸策为你挑选的咖啡……
t_en: The café's "Lightpaw Express Delivery" arrives at Dovebrook-surfing speed. With the deadline looming, you savor this rare moment of rest... and the coffee Furbulitzer picked just for you...

t_ch: 冰辣椒摩卡
t_en: Iced Chili Pepper Mocha

t_ch: 咕嘟咕嘟，一股辛辣的味道顺着你的喉管俯冲而下，太阳穴开始渗出微微的薄汗——这不是普通的辣，是魔鬼椒在岩浆里淬炼过的口感，如果这玩意也能叫作咖啡的话……
t_en: Glug, glug... A searing heat dives down your throat. Sweat beads at your temples. This isn't normal spicy, this is devil pepper forged in magma, if this thing even qualifies as coffee...

t_ch: 章鱼眼睛爆珠啵啵
t_en: Octopus Eye Poppin' Boba

t_ch: 一口咬下混在在海盐味道中的小料，感受着那股Q弹中带着胶质的韧劲，你意识到，真正的深渊，从勇敢咬开第一颗章鱼眼睛爆珠那刻起，刚刚向你眨了眨眼。
t_en: You bite down through the sea-salt mix. Chewy. Gelatinous. Disturbingly resilient. In that instant, you realize: the Abyss has just winked at you, the moment you dared bite your first octopus eye.

t_ch: 醋昆布冷萃
t_en: Vinegar Kelp Cold Brew

t_ch: 你隐约想起有人曾说过醋昆布像老爷爷腋下的味道，而配合了冷萃的醋昆布则像擦拭过腋下的汗巾在水里泡了三个昼夜的味道，猛啜一口，令人目眩神迷……
t_en: You vaguely recall someone saying vinegar kelp smells like an old man's armpit. Cold-brewed, it's more like an armpit towel soaked for three straight days. One hard gulp is enough to make the world spin...
Blergh...

t_ch: 社长大人，你怎么了？怎么翻白眼了哩？
t_en: Boss... what's wrong? Why're your eyes rolling back like that!?

t_ch: 感谢曾被姬子的咖啡折磨过的钢铁肠胃，在关键时刻救了你。
t_en: Thank the iron stomach you forged surviving Himeko's coffee. It saved you when it mattered.

t_ch: 上普狸策的咖啡车这种事…不会再有下次了！
t_en: Getting on Furbulitzer's coffee cart... never again!

t_ch: 社长大人，社长大人，大事不好哩！
t_en: Boss! We've got a situation!

t_ch: {TEXTJOIN#254}论坛上出现了一篇奇怪的帖子，刻意引导网友误解我们和不死途侦探的关系，还艾特了杂志社官号，社长大人快来看看是怎么一回事哩。
t_en: There's a weird post on the {TEXTJOIN#254} forum deliberately twisting our relationship with the detective Ashveil. They even tagged the magazine's official account. President, you need to see this.

t_ch: 立刻查看。
t_en: Check it out right away.

t_ch: 细说怎么误解的。
t_en: Elaborate on how they twisted our relationship.

t_ch: 总之…总之就是说我们可能和他私下勾兑，然后这样这样，那样那样，一堆乱七八糟的，解释不清楚哩！
t_en: Anyway... Anyway, they're saying we're secretly colluding with him, doing this and that, scheming behind the scenes! A whole mess of stuff that's impossible to explain!

t_ch: 你的解释也很让人误会啊！
t_en: Your explanation is making it worse!

t_ch: 再来两毛钱的。
t_en: Gimme another two cents' worth.

t_ch: 社长大人就别开玩笑哩，十万火急，您亲自去看看吧。
t_en: Boss, this is no time for jokes! It's an emergency! You'd better go check it out yourself!

t_ch: 暂时不处理。
t_en: Let's not deal with this for now.

t_ch: 哇…不愧是社长大人，有着雷虬塔崩于眼前面不改色的气魄哩……
t_en: Wow... as expected of our president. Totally unfazed, even with chaos unfolding right before your eyes...

t_ch: 尤狸安遵命，这件事——咱们暂不处理！让那些讨厌的声音自己发酵会儿哩！
t_en: Roger that. We won't be dealing with this matter just yet! Let the noise stew for a while!

t_ch: 真是太过分分了，社长大人，咱们到底要怎么处理呢？
t_en: This is outrageous, Boss. What are we actually going to do?

t_ch: 表明立场免惹麻烦。
t_en: Publicly take your stand to avoid trouble.

t_ch: 懂了！小浣熊冷傲退黑子！那么…我就这样写……
t_en: Got it! Raccoon coolly shuts down the haters! Then... I'll write it like this...

t_ch: 谨代表《狸狸周刊》杂志社严肃澄清：本刊始终保持中立客观的调查立场，绝无任何私联，如果点赞量超过<unbreak>500</unbreak>条，本刊保留诉讼诽谤的权利。
t_en: On behalf of Furbobo Weekly, we solemnly clarify: Our publication has always maintained a neutral and objective investigative stance, with absolutely no private dealings. Should likes exceed 500, we reserve the right to pursue legal action for defamation.

t_ch: 就发「我们的关系被发现了！」
t_en: Just post "We got found out!"

t_ch: 这这这，这样说真的好吗？
t_en: I—I—Is it really okay to say that!?

t_ch: 住一栋楼的关系怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong with living in the same building?

t_ch: 要懂得新闻爆点。
t_en: You have to understand news hooks.

t_ch: 我懂了，不愧是精通传媒之道的社长大人，那么，我就这样写……
t_en: I get it now! You really are a master of media, Boss! Alright then, I'll write it this way...

t_ch: 没错，本刊和不死途侦探事务所的关系是…一起分摊同一栋楼的水电费的关系，被大家发现了！
t_en: That's right, our publication and the Ashveil Detective Agency share a... relationship of splitting the utility bills for the same building. You've all found us out!

t_ch: 这种不实消息直接封禁。
t_en: Ban this kind of fake news immediately.

t_ch: 收到！不愧是雷厉风行、铁腕手段的社长大人，我马上从后台查封这篇帖子！
t_en: Roger that! As expected of our decisive, iron-fisted boss! I'll take down that post from the backend right away!

t_ch: 社长大人，佛狸想来咨询一下职业规划，您的时间是否方便呶？
t_en: Boss, I would like some advice on career planning. Do you have a moment?

t_ch: 如果不占时间的话没问题。
t_en: Sure, as long as it doesn't take long.

t_ch: 谢谢社长大人呶，我会尽快呶。
t_en: Thank you, Boss! I'll be quick!

t_ch: 你不会是打算跳槽了吧？！
t_en: You're not thinking of leaving us, are you!?

t_ch: 能让我放心摸鱼的公司可是不多见了呶，我很珍惜这份工作呶。
t_en: Companies where I can slack off in peace are pretty rare, y'know. I really treasure this gig.

t_ch: 我也没规划…你问错人了。
t_en: I don't have a plan either... you're asking the wrong person.

t_ch: 原来您也和我一样…可您是我的领导嘛，我也只能找到您了呶。
t_en: So you're in the same boat as me... but you're my boss, so you're the only one I can ask.

t_ch: 呶呶呶…我也在狸狸通信工作很久了，却总是被父母说没有一点职业规划，缺乏目标。
t_en: I've worked at Furbobocom for ages now, but my parents keep saying I have no career plan, no direction in life.

t_ch: 可是佛狸每天上班就是为了下班，感觉目标还是蛮明确的呶…？
t_en: But I clock in just so I can clock out. That seems like a pretty clear goal to me...

t_ch: 别担心，你的目标规划很明确。
t_en: Don't worry, you've got your goals all mapped out.

t_ch: 谢谢社长大人呶，我有信心哩！
t_en: Thank you so much, Boss! I've got this!

t_ch: 要是父母问起来，我就说是您说的。
t_en: If my parents ask, I'll just say you told me so.

t_ch: 你认为我作为老板该怎么回答？
t_en: What do you think I should say, as your boss?

t_ch: 啊？佛狸可不是领导心腹，不懂这个那个的。
t_en: Huh? I'm not boss's confidant. I don't know all that fancy stuff.

t_ch: 说不定幻太子知道，我等会问问它呶。
t_en: Maybe Paraprince knows? I'll ask them later.

t_ch: 你确实需要多去接触下职场了。
t_en: You really do need more real workplace exposure.

t_ch: 但…这就是我接触职场之后才得出的结论呶。
t_en: But... this is exactly the conclusion I reached after exposing myself to the workforce.

t_ch: 咦？是我把打工狸文学看少了吗？
t_en: Huh? Have I not been reading enough "working-furbo" literature?

t_ch: 呶呶呶…我好像已经明白了，太复杂的思考不适合佛狸，我还是继续深耕摸鱼领域吧。
t_en: I think I get it now. Deep thinking isn't for me. I should stick to what I do best: mastering the art of slacking off.

t_ch: …原本我还准备了一些职场难题也浪费呶。不过，社长大人我还是想听听您的答案呶！
t_en: ...I even prepared some workplace brain-teasers, but I guess those are gonna go to waste. Still, Boss, I really wanna hear your answer!

t_ch: ——假如社长您现在是打工狸，您有4杯咖啡，却来了5个领导，您该怎么办？
t_en: ...Let's say you're a working furbo with four cups of coffee, but five managers showed up. What do you do?

t_ch: 有意思，我给你个方案。
t_en: Interesting. I've got a solution.

t_ch: 考验职场情商的时刻到了。
t_en: Time to put that workplace EQ to the test.

t_ch: …再买一杯呗？
t_en: ...Buy one more cup?

t_ch: 都有5位领导了，还买不起咖啡么？再去买一杯就是了嘛。
t_en: You've got five managers now and you can't afford a coffee? Just go buy another.

t_ch: 社长大人说…{F#她}{M#他}买咖啡……
t_en: The boss said... {F#she}{M#he} should go buy coffee...

t_ch: 一起来抽卡吧！
t_en: Let's draw lots!

t_ch: 传统分咖啡的方式，好无趣；加入抽卡概率的分咖啡，好好玩。
t_en: Splitting coffee the old way is boring. Adding lucky draw odds to coffee distribution? Now that's what we call fun.

t_ch: 社长大人说…喜欢抽卡…好好玩……
t_en: Boss said... {F#she}{M#he} likes lucky draws... really fun...

t_ch: 简单的数学题…
t_en: Easy math...

t_ch: 平均分，再分一杯…有点难是吧？让5个领导减少1个人，应该不难。
t_en: Dividing things equally... and then finding one more share... that's a bit tough, right? Well, cutting one out of five managers shouldn't be that hard.

t_ch: 社长大人说…{F#她}{M#他}有超暴力级数学思维……
t_en: The boss said... {F#she}{M#he} has absurdly aggressive math instincts...

t_ch: 这不就是热门面试题吗？
t_en: Isn't this just a classic interview question?

t_ch: 没错，据说能看出一只狸在职场的情商呶，我很好奇呶。
t_en: Exactly. They say it shows a furbo's workplace EQ, y'know? I'm super curious!

t_ch: …你来面试领导就已经很说明问题了。
t_en: ...The fact that you're interviewing leadership already says it all.

t_ch: 佛狸，而你该怎么办呢？
t_en: Furli, what are you gonna do?

t_ch: 社长大人又在反客为主了……
t_en: The boss is flipping the script again...

t_ch: 呃…佛狸的话，应该是什么都不会做吧。
t_en: Uh... if it were me, I'd probably do nothing.

t_ch: 那你最好闭着眼、摸索着将杯子放在桌子上，大家应该会让让狸的。
t_en: Then you'd better close your eyes and fumble the cup onto the table. Everyone will make way for a furbo.

t_ch: 这次谈话之后，佛狸学到了很多呶…社长大人，小狸我这就告退呶。
t_en: I have learned much from this conversation, y'know... I shall now take my leave, Boss.

t_ch: 把题目发到工作群吧，我想看。
t_en: Post the question in the work chat. I want to see it.

t_ch: 呶呶呶…收到。
t_en: Beep-boop... Got it.

t_ch: 社长大人！方便吗，有几个问题想问问您！
t_en: Boss! Got a minute? I've got a few questions for you!

t_ch: 请问吧。
t_en: Ask away.

t_ch: 谢谢社长大人。
t_en: Thank you, Boss.

t_ch: 快点吧。
t_en: Hurry it up.

t_ch: 好嘞，放心吧大人。
t_en: You got it, Boss.

t_ch: 《狸狸周刊》受邀成为二相乐园时尚节的特邀媒体了。其中有一个小环节，询问杂志社主编的包里平时装了些什么东西，要和大家分享。
t_en: Furbobo Weekly has been invited as a featured media partner for Planarcadia's Fashion Festival. One of the segments involves asking the magazine's President to share what's usually inside their bag.

t_ch: 我平时不背包。
t_en: I don't carry a bag.

t_ch: 社长大人说…真正的时尚人士…不背包……
t_en: The boss said... true fashionistas... don't carry bags...

t_ch: 四个字：伸手要钱。
t_en: Four letters: W-I-C-K.

t_ch: 身份信息卡、手机、钥匙、钱包。别的无所谓。
t_en: Wallet, ID, Cellphone, Keys. Nothing else matters.

t_ch: 社长大人说…潮人的包包里装什么…你别管……
t_en: The boss said... what a trendsetter keeps in their bag... is none of your business...

t_ch: 你要是这么记录的话，我实在是有点太狂妄了，完全不顾其他媒体的感受。
t_en: If you write it down like that, I'm going to come off way too cocky, like I couldn't care less how the other outlets feel.

t_ch: 嗷…社长大人，反正没有人真正地打开过您的包，要不…您现编几个我去交差。
t_en: Ugh... Boss, no one's actually ever looked inside your bag anyway, how about... you just make something up, and I'll call it a day?

t_ch: 我编撰个时尚单品吧。
t_en: I'll make up a fashion item, then.

t_ch: 期待社长大人的创作。
t_en: Can't wait to see what you come up with.

t_ch: 三月七同款相机。
t_en: March 7th's signature camera.

t_ch: 社长大人说…只认可…三月七的时尚观……
t_en: The boss said... the only fashion sense worth respecting... is March 7th's...

t_ch: 我姥姥的大花袄。
t_en: My grandma's big floral quilted jacket.

t_en: Other people "add a little spice." You're dumping in oil, salt, MSG, vinegar, soy sauce AND chili flakes to top it all off.

t_en: Please don't copy me...

t_ch: 谢谢您的回答，以上就是「最不时尚奖」候选人的采访。
t_en: Thanks for your answers. That wraps up our interview with the nominee for "Least Fashionable Award."

t_ch: 你小子不许给我挖坑！
t_en: How dare you set me up, punk!

t_ch: 哔嘟，浣熊大人，我们收到了一些后台私信反馈，有些是针对论坛上的贴子，有些是针对我们杂志社，说什么的都有。
t_en: Beep-boop, Great Raccoon, we've gotten a bunch of DMs from the backend. Some are about that forum post, some are about our magazine... it's a whole mixed bag.

t_ch: 浣熊大人帮我们看看，一起想想怎么回复？
t_en: Great Raccoon, help us take a look, and let's figure out how to reply?

t_ch: 反正闲着也是闲着…
t_en: Might as well.

t_ch: 行，一起看看。
t_en: Sure, let's check it out together.

t_ch: 那咱们开始吧！第一条后台反馈，IP地址来自绘世学院——
t_en: Alright then, let's get started! First message, IP address traced back to Graphia Academy...

t_ch: 「我举报这个帖子，发帖人说『这破学校害死我了，到处都是笨蛋，跟猪圈一样！』，我感觉受到了严重的冒犯，希望狸狸通信协助整改。」
t_en: "I'm reporting this post. The poster said, 'This trash school is killing me, it's full of idiots — basically a pigsty!' I feel seriously offended and hope Furbobocom can help rectify this."

t_ch: 浣熊大人觉得应该怎么建议发帖人修改呢？
t_en: What does the Great Raccoon think the poster should change it to?

t_ch: 「此地囚我少年意气！」
t_en: "This place imprisons my youthful spirit!"

t_ch: 实在是文雅而有力，同时不会冒犯到别人的控诉啵，就这么改！
t_en: Elegant and forceful, and it won't offend anyone either, boop! Let's go with that!

t_ch: 「好无助，学校里数我最聪明。」
t_en: "I feel so helpless, and I'm the smartest one in school."

t_ch: 太厉害了，深藏不露的嘲讽啊，就这么改！
t_en: Brilliant! A quiet, vicious burn. Let's roll with that!

t_ch: 不用改，谁被冒犯到说明谁…
t_en: No need to change it. If someone gets offended, that just means they...

t_ch: 您说得对，谎言不是利刃，真相才是快刀。不是笨蛋不会受伤，那这条咱们直接跳过啵。
t_en: You're right. Lies aren't a blade, the truth is the sharpest cut. And if you're not an idiot, you won't get hurt. Let's skip this one then, boop.

t_ch: 第二条后台反馈，IP地址来自「世界尽头」酒馆——
t_en: Second message, IP traced to the "World's End" Tavern...

t_ch: 「我看了论坛上的黄金马桶帖子才去坐的，结果被传送到了酒馆的下水道里，接下来的事不说了，总之，我要求狸狸通信赔偿我清洗费和精神损失费！」请锐评。
t_en: "I sat on it after seeing the golden toilet post on the forum, only to get teleported into the tavern's sewers. I won't go into what happened next, but long story short, I'm demanding Furbobocom compensate me for cleaning fees AND emotional damages!" Please comment.

t_ch: 「矢到淋头！好福气。」
t_en: "A golden opportunity is raining down on you! Truly, you're blessed."

t_ch: 好福气啊好福气，这福气给我…啊，我不敢要啵。
t_en: What a blessing, what a blessing... give it to me... Ah, no, I wouldn't dare take it.

t_ch: 「找酒馆赔，我们不管。」
t_en: "Go get the tavern to compensate you. Not our problem."

t_ch: 哔哔，同意！咱们要硬气一点，不能被讹诈啵。
t_en: Beep-beep, agreed! We need to stand our ground and not let ourselves get scammed, boop.

t_ch: 「鸽川大舞台，有胆你就来。」
t_en: "Dovebrook Grand Stage — if you've got guts, bring it on."

t_ch: 哼哼，懂了，尽管上门来讨教吧，咱们狸猫一族也不是吃素的啵。
t_en: Hehe, gotcha! Come at me anytime! We furbos aren't pushovers, y'know!

t_ch: 第三条后台反馈，IP地址来自二维市治安局——
t_en: Third message, IP traced to Duomension City Security Bureau...

t_ch: 「没别的意思，只是把狸狸通信当树洞吐槽一下。每天上班都有人对我说『你是真的狗啊！』很烦，到底什么意思呢？是对我工作能力的否定吗？忐忑忐忑忐忑……」
t_en: "I don't mean anything by it, just using Furbobocom as a vent box. Every day at work, someone tells me, 'You're such a dog!' It's so annoying! What does that even mean? Are they insulting my work performance? I'm freaking out..."

t_ch: 有没有一种可能…
t_en: Is it possible that...

t_ch: 你其实是一只犬灵？
t_en: ...you're actually a woofae?

t_ch: 啊！原来如此，浣熊大人太有新闻嗅觉了啵，这条必须列入我的发稿素材库啵。
t_en: Ah! So that's it! Great Raccoon, your nose for news is unreal, boop. This one's going straight into my story archive, boop.

t_ch: 今天的后台回复时间就到这里，感谢浣熊大人的解答啵。
t_en: That's all for today's Q&A session, thanks for the answers, Great Raccoon!

t_ch: 浣熊大人，您还记得咱们的外派记者比狸比狸吗？
t_en: Great Raccoon, do you remember our field reporter, Furblix?

t_ch: 最近《狸狸周刊》的长视频和直播业务正在由它试点运营，吸引了不少关注哩。
t_en: It's been piloting the long-form video and livestreaming business for Furbobo Weekly lately. It caught quite a bit of attention.

t_en: Furblix says the fans' comments on public screens are all about how they want to see Baseball Raccoon, so! It wants to start a livestream battle with you to get more fans!

t_en: A livestream battle? Interesting.

t_ch: 考验我人气值的时候到了？我现在就来。
t_en: Time to put my popularity to the test? I'm on my way.

t_ch: 有点忙，等会再来吧。
t_en: I'm a bit busy right now. I'll come back later.

t_ch: 只做最优质的内容
t_en: Only top-tier content.

t_en: It's empty here. Why not write something?

t_en: →Fly free, Little Gray! Big bro's got your back←

t_en: If you're not afraid, I'll take some time to tell you about the supernatural happenings in the magazine.

t_en: Supernatural happenings? Let's hear them.

t_ch: 等会再听吧。
t_en: Maybe later.

t_ch: 好的哩，等社长大人有空我再来找您。
t_en: Alrighty! I'll come back when you're free.

t_ch: 浣熊大人，报告一则消息：最近《狸狸周刊》的年终绩效盘点就快要到了。
t_en: Great Raccoon, a message for you: The year-end performance review for Furbobo Weekly is just around the corner.

t_ch: 请选择您的绩效评定策略哩！
t_en: Please select your performance review strategy!

t_ch: 杂志社还有绩效一说？
t_en: Performance review in the magazine?

t_ch: 虽然虚照社长在任的时候，的确是没有的。但从专业的管理学角度来说，还是有必要启用的哩！
t_en: Although we certainly didn't have that when Director Nihilux was in charge... from a professional management perspective, it's absolutely necessary to implement it!

t_ch: 狸猫们干活也讲绩效吗？
t_en: Do furbos have performance reviews, too?

t_ch: 是啊是啊，咱们虽然规模小，也是受二相乐园《灵物保护法》规定的正规企业哩！
t_en: Yeah, yeah! We might be small, but we're a legit business operating under Planarcadia's Faekind Protection Act!

t_ch: 流程的事情就交给我普狸策来办吧，浣熊大人只需要制定决策就好哩！也就是——您决定以什么样的策略给狸猫们评定工作成果呢？
t_en: Leave the procedural stuff to me, Great Raccoon! All you need to do is make the executive decisions! Which means... What kind of strategy have you decided on for evaluating our work?

t_ch: 以宽松随性的方式评定。
t_en: Judged based on loose and casual standards.

t_en: Furbulitzer, let me test you: Is the value of an employee's work really determined by the boss's evaluation?

t_ch: 这这这…那我们的工作价值由谁说了算呢？
t_en: Th—Then... who gets to decide the value of our work?

t_ch: 当然是<color=#dbc291ff>「自己」</color>啊！二相乐园千禧年前后的年轻人都是宽松的一代，所以我决定：狸猫们给自己打多少分，我就评多少分。
t_en: Of course, it's <color=#dbc291ff>yourselves</color>! The youth of Planarcadia around the millennium were the "relaxed generation," so I've decided: Whatever score you give yourselves, that's the score I'll give you.

t_ch: 原来是这样，我们要把狸生的主动权掌握在自己手里哩……
t_en: So that's how it is... We must take our destiny into our own hands...

t_ch: 以共享优秀的方式评定。
t_en: Judged based on shared excellence.

t_ch: 所谓绩效，不过是资本剥削员工的名义。普狸策，你的觉悟还不够高啊。
t_en: So-called "performance" is just a pretext for capitalist exploitation. You're still lacking some insight, Furbulitzer.

t_ch: 啊啊，浣熊大人教训得是！是我该学习先进的社会科学狸论了哩！
t_en: Aaaah, you're right, Great Raccoon! It is I who must study advanced social science furb-eories!

t_ch: 《狸狸周刊》奉行「有福同享主义」，大家<color=#dbc291ff>共享「优秀」</color>吧！不够的奖金从我的存钱罐里扣。
t_en: Furbobo Weekly operates on the shared joy principle, so let's all share in this excellence! If we're short on prize money, it's coming out of my deposit.

t_ch: 太好了哩，谢谢老板！！老板好大方！
t_en: Great! Thanks, boss! You're so generous!

t_ch: 以科学有效的方式评定。
t_en: Judged based on a scientific and effective manner.

t_en: It's my first time being a President. I wanted to ask you first: What does a proper, scientific evaluation process usually look like?

t_ch: 一般都是员工先完成自评，老板再找大家分别面谈，最后结合大家的自评给出正式的评定结果哩。
t_en: Usually, the employees complete a self-evaluation first, then the boss chats with everyone one-on-one, and finally, the official results are given based on those self-evaluations.

t_ch: 不错，我懒得想别的办法了，我们也这么执行吧。
t_en: Not bad. I can't be bothered to think of anything else, so let's just roll with that.

t_ch: 哇，就这么决定了吗？太效率了哩。
t_en: Whoa, just like that? Talk about efficiency!

t_ch: 以社长优先的方式评定。
t_en: Judged based on the President's priorities.

t_ch: 首先我会给我自己打出「非常满意」，因为我从接手《狸狸周刊》以来，简直是一心扑在杂志社上，成功让《狸狸周刊》的订阅量翻了好几番。
t_en: First off, I'm rating myself "Very Satisfied." Since taking over Furbobo Weekly, I've practically poured my heart and soul into the magazine, and successfully multiplied our subscription numbers several times over.

t_ch: 浣熊大人说得没错！《狸狸周刊》离了您是得倒闭。
t_en: Great Raccoon is absolutely right! Furbobo Weekly would be done for without you.

t_ch: 至于狸猫们…我听说，星际和平公司有那种夺取基层员工绩效点的传统……
t_en: As for the furbos... I've heard the IPC has a tradition of seizing Performance Points from entry-level employees...

t_ch: 啊啊啊，难道说……
t_en: Aaaah! Could it be...

t_ch: 此时此刻，我终于理解了曾经的敌人。
t_en: Right this moment, I finally understand my past enemies.

t_ch: 普狸策，请转告大家：你们的就是我的，我的还是我的。下一年也狠狠地被我奴役吧！么么么♥～
t_en: Furbulitzer, please pass this on: What's yours is mine, and what's mine is still mine. Work yourself to the bone for me next year, too! Mwah ♥~

t_ch: 收到！我马上把浣熊大人的绩效评定策略同步给狸猫们哩。
t_en: Roger! I'll sync your performance review strategy to the furbos right away, Great Raccoon!

t_ch: 主人主人，胡构狸来哩～～
t_en: Master, Master! Furbricato is here~

t_ch: 主人是否偶尔会觉得办公室稍显滞闷不通风～～需不需要胡构狸给您提供一点提神的小故事哩～～
t_en: Do you ever find the office a bit stuffy and stagnant, Master~ Would you like Furbricato to tell you a little story to lift your spirits~

t_ch: 你出现在这里，已经够提神了。
t_en: Just you showing up here is enough to lift my spirits.

t_ch: 谢谢主人对我的肯定哩～～
t_en: Thank you for your praise, Master~

t_ch: 还是有点不习惯称呼我为主人。
t_en: I'm still not quite used to being called Master.

t_ch: 啊，这个是我还活着的时候人们对「老板」的旧式称呼～～请不要往别的方向曲解哩～～
t_en: Ah, that's just an old-fashioned term for "Boss" from back when I was still alive~ Please don't misinterpret it~

t_ch: 胡构狸接下来要讲的故事～～在二相乐园有个经典称呼叫作<color=#dbc291ff>「克拉肯汤」</color>～～
t_en: The story I'm about to tell~ goes by a classic name in Planarcadia: <color=#dbc291ff>Kraken Broth</color>~

t_ch: 我会讲一段留有悬念的情节作为开头～～而主人需要逐渐通过排除法猜出它的谜底，也就是「汤底」哩～～
t_en: I'll start with a suspenseful opening~ Then, you need to use the process of elimination to guess the answer... otherwise known as the "broth"~

t_ch: 那么开始了！
t_en: Here we go!

t_ch: 有只狸猫在原本定好的截稿日开了天窗～～但是《狸狸周刊》的狸都对它说「谢谢狸！谢谢狸！」。请问这是为什么哩～～
t_en: A furbo failed to meet the deadline and submitted only a rough draft. The staff at Furbobo Weekly kept shouting at it: "Thanx! Thanx!" Why~

t_ch: 按照惯例，先让我思考…
t_en: As usual, let me think first...

t_ch: （得先思考思考这句话里存在哪些可疑的地方……）
t_en: (I need to think about what's suspicious about this sentence...)

t_ch: 被刻意隐藏掉的信息…
t_en: Information that was deliberately hidden...

t_ch: （胡构狸没有说这只狸猫是谁，所以这里存在可以发挥联想的「空白」……）
t_en: (Furbricato didn't specify who this furbo is, so there's a "blank space" here that can be used to connect...)

t_ch: 前后叙述矛盾的地方…
t_en: The contradictions in the sentence...

t_ch: （如果是自家的狸猫拖稿，《狸狸周刊》的狸猫是不可能感谢它的，从这个角度来说，答案已经清晰了……）
t_en: (If one of our own furbos missed the deadline, the furbos at Furbobo Weekly would never be thanking it. From that perspective, the answer is already clear...)

t_ch: 等一等…谁被开了？
t_en: Wait a minute... who got cut?

t_ch: （不错，我真是机智，<color=#dbc291ff>开了天窗</color>，这里存在一层「歧义」……）
t_en: (Nice, I'm a genius. <color=#dbc291ff>Cut the page</color>... There's an ambiguity here...)

t_ch: 思考完毕，给出答案！
t_en: Got it all figured out. Here's the answer!

t_ch: 你自信满满地给出了「汤底」，那就是……
t_en: With absolute confidence, you present the "broth." And that is...

t_ch: 这只狸猫就叫「谢谢狸」！
t_en: This furbo is called "Thanx"!

t_ch: 啊啊啊～～因为它叫「谢谢狸」～～所以狸猫们说「谢谢狸！谢谢狸！」其实是在大声催促它交稿吗～～
t_en: Aaaah~ Because its name is "Thanx"~ So when the furbos are shouting "Thanx! Thanx!" they're actually urging it to finish the draft~

t_ch: 这只狸猫是竞争对手家的狸猫！
t_en: This furbo belongs to the competition!

t_ch: 啊啊啊～～因为它不是《狸狸周刊》的员工～～所以「谢谢你的对手，他们的失败，才让你显得更加成功」吗～～
t_en: Aaaahhh~ Because it's not a Furbobo Weekly employee~ So "Thank your opponent for their failure, for that makes you look more successful," right~

t_ch: 拖稿的是「天窗」，它被开了！
t_en: The furbo who failed to meet the deadline is Page, so Page got cut!

t_ch: 啊啊啊～～未曾设想的角度～～的确，从来没说过《狸狸周刊》的员工不能是狸猫之外的幻造种哩～～
t_en: Aaah~ I never thought of it that way~ It's true. Nobody ever said employees at Furbobo Weekly couldn't be imagenae other than furbos~

t_ch: 主人实在是太聪明哩～～从胡编乱造…啊不，从借题发挥这方面来说～～我胡构狸也要承让三分哩～～
t_en: Master, you're just too smart~ When it comes to making things up... ah, I mean, improvising on the spot~ even I, Furbricato, have to concede defeat~

t_ch: 那么——现在揭开我的汤底！其实是「天冷了，狸猫们在屋子里烤火，险些一氧化碳中毒，多亏了开天窗的狸哩！」～～
t_en: And so, time to reveal my broth! It's actually: The office heater was releasing carbon monoxide during the cold day. Everyone was saved because the rough draft aired out the room!

t_ch: 哦…原来是这样啊…
t_en: Oh... so this is how it is...

t_ch: 嘁…没比我高明啊！
t_en: Tch... My version is much better!

t_ch: 哎哎～～我承认～～主人的「克拉肯汤」能力更胜一筹～～等我回去修炼修炼，再来和您玩哩～～
t_en: Alright, alright~ I admit it~ Master's Kraken Broth ability is superior~ I'm gonna go back and train a bit, then I'll come back and play with you again~

t_ch: 好奇怪啊老板咱们的工作群里突然多了一个账号发了一大堆垃圾广告信息简直刷屏了话比我还多连我比狸比狸都无法忍受这种低质量内容了
t_en: It's so weird boss a new account suddenly popped up in our work chat and started spamming a bunch of trash ads, it's totally flooding the screen they talk even more than I do even I Furblix can't stand such low-quality content.

t_ch: 垃圾广告信息？
t_en: Trash ads?

t_ch: 就是那种你不搭理它会变本加厉越发越多的广告简直乱七八糟什么都有比鸽川区进行城市美化行动之前的电线杆上到处都是的牛皮藓小广告还恐怖哩
t_en: It's the kind of spam that gets worse if you ignore it multiplying like crazy, total chaos selling anything and everything, it's even scarier than those illicit flyers plastered all over the telephone poles in Dovebrook District before the city beautification campaign.

t_ch: 突然多出的账号？
t_en: An account that appeared out of nowhere?

t_ch: 不知道什么时候加到工作群里来的号连个头像也没有反正我是一点印象也没有哩普狸策也不知道是怎么回事所以暂时还不敢把它移除群聊哩
t_en: I have no idea when this account was added to the work group chat and it doesn't even have an avatar anyway, I don't remember it at all Furbulitzer doesn't know what's going on either so I don't dare remove it from the group chat for now.

t_ch: 老板是不是还没看到啊快来看看吧
t_en: Did the boss not see it yet? Hurry up and take a look!

t_ch: 老板啊还记得上次群聊里突然冒出的发了很多垃圾信息的账号吗我们找到原因了哩！
t_en: Hey boss remember that account that popped up in the group chat and started spamming a bunch of trash? We found out why.

t_ch: 它被举报销号了？
t_en: Did it get reported and banned?

t_ch: 我的呼叫成功了？
t_en: Did my call work?

t_ch: 您来群里看看就知道了哩！
t_en: Just check the group chat and you'll see!

t_ch: 社长大人，一大清早的不知道为了什么事，普狸策在杂志社门口和人吵起来了哩！
t_en: Boss! First thing in the morning, Furbulitzer got into an argument with someone right outside the magazine office. No idea what it was about!

t_ch: 咱们快去看看吧！
t_en: C'mon, let's go check it out!

t_ch: 浣熊大人，除了我和幻太子，您还没怎么见过咱们《狸狸周刊》的其他员工吧，不少狸猫也盼着早点认识新社长哩。
t_en: Great Raccoon, besides me and Paraprince, you haven't really met the rest of the Furbobo Weekly staff, right? A lot of furbos have been dying to meet their new boss.

t_ch: 我这就把您拉到工作群里，隆重介绍一番！
t_en: I'll add you to the work group chat right now and introduce you properly!

t_ch: 现在就去。
t_en: On my way.

t_ch: 让他们再吵一会。
t_en: Let them bicker a little longer.

t_ch: 这是重振咱们《狸狸周刊》的大事哦，社长准备好了吗？
t_en: This is a huge deal for reviving our Furbobo Weekly! So, President, are you ready?

t_ch: 准备好了。
t_en: Ready.

t_ch: 稍等一会。
t_en: Hold on for a moment.

t_ch: 工作…干不完呶！该招新人呶！我的前期工作已经准备就绪，就等老板有空了！
t_en: So much work to do! We need to hire new staff! I've got everything prepped and ready to go, just waiting for you, president!

t_ch: 有空。
t_en: I'm free.

t_ch: 等我排一下档期。
t_en: Let me check my schedule.

t_ch: 我刚还在工位上看到普狸策？
t_en: I saw Furbulitzer at the workstation just now?

t_ch: 啊…它说是气不过回办公室，喘两口气，再继续吵。
t_en: Oh that... He said he got too worked up, so he came back to the office, took a breather, and went straight back out to continue arguing.

t_ch: 这里…怎么会有帕姆的玩偶？
t_en: Why would there be a Pom-Pom doll here?

t_ch: 这是一个帕姆玩偶，与你在列车上见到的别无二致…但是它为什么出现在这里？
t_en: This is a Pom-Pom doll, identical to the Pom-Pom you see on the Express... But why would it be here?

t_ch: 碰一碰它。
t_en: Poke it.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 它的手感毛茸茸软乎乎，甚至比列车长本人更令你爱不释手，不过……
t_en: It has a soft and fluffy texture, almost more endearing and adorable than the conductor themselves, but...

t_ch: 哎呀！
t_en: Ow!

t_ch: 被你好一阵蹂躏后，玩偶变成了一个…皮皮西人？
t_en: After you give it a good fondling, the doll transforms into... a Pepeshi?

t_ch: 你耍赖！怎么可能有人识破我的伪装？
t_en: Cheater! How could you see through my disguise like that?

t_ch: 什么伪装？
t_en: What disguise?

t_ch: 原来你不是玩偶？
t_en: So, you're not a doll?

t_ch: 我是来自凯尔萨斯星的红泥粘土人，叫作杰诺。刚才的玩偶是我拟态出来的！
t_en: I am a Red Clayform of Kalsas, my name is Jno. That doll form just now was my mimicry!

t_ch: 从来没人识破过我的拟态，你是怎么做到的？
t_en: No one has ever seen through my mimicry before. How did you do that?

t_ch: 我打小就聪明。
t_en: I have always been a genius.

t_ch: 是嘛…不对，你肯定是蒙的！有本事就再比试一场。
t_en: Oh really... No, you must have cheated somehow! Let's try again if you think you've got the skills!

t_ch: 你找的位置太奇怪了。
t_en: You picked a strange place to hide.

t_ch: 是嘛…不对，你肯定是蒙的！有本事就再比试一场。
t_en: Oh really... No, you must have cheated somehow! Let's try again if you think you've got the skills!

t_ch: 我只是随手摸摸…
t_en: I just happened to touch you in passing...

t_ch: 好哇，我就知道你是蒙的！有本事就再比试一场。
t_en: I knew it! I knew you cheated! Let's try again if you think you've got the skills!

t_ch: 这次我会变成不一样的东西…你就站在这里，不要走动。
t_en: I will transform into something else this time... You stand here and don't move!

t_ch: 奇怪的皮皮西离开了。
t_en: The strange Pepeshi left.

t_ch: 这是一面鼓，人类最古老的乐器之一。虽然不知道它为什么出现在这里，但它敲起来想必非常好听。
t_en: This is a drum, one of humanity's oldest musical instruments. Though you have no idea why it would be here, you know it will make a great sound when played.

t_ch: 敲一敲它。
t_en: Bang the drum.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 鼓发出了一连串打击乐声。无论触感还是外形，它都表现得像一面普通的鼓…你沉浸在节奏中，忘我地拍打着鼓面。
t_en: The drum erupts into a wave of percussion. Everything about this drum from texture to shape seems completely normal... You lose yourself in the rhythm and continue banging on the drum.

t_ch: 哎，疼疼疼！
t_en: Ow, that hurts, oww!

t_ch: 在你的摧残中，鼓投降般变回了皮皮西的模样。
t_en: Under your savage beatings, the drum surrenders and transforms back into its Pepeshi form.

t_ch: 找就找嘛，下手那么重干什么……
t_en: Okay, okay, you found me! There's no need to beat me up like that...

t_ch: 话说回来，这次你又是怎么认出我的？
t_en: Anyway, how did you recognize me this time?

t_ch: 我的第六感很强。
t_en: I have a strong sixth sense.

t_ch: 你摆的位置太奇怪了。
t_en: You chose a strange place to hide.

t_ch: 我只是随手打鼓…
t_en: I just wanted to bang the drum...

t_ch: 唔…好强，果然还是我太弱小了吗……
t_en: Oof... You are too powerful. Am I really just no match for you...?

t_ch: 再来一次！再给我最后一次机会。这次我一定能变出你无法察觉的完美拟态！
t_en: Let's go again! Give me one, final chance! This time I will create the perfect mimicry that you will never be able to detect!

t_ch: 奇怪的皮皮西蹦跳着离开了…
t_en: The strange Pepeshi hopped into the distance...

t_ch: 你打开了眼前的宝箱——里面什么也没有。你从没遇见过这样「任性」的宝箱。
t_en: You open the chest before you — but find nothing inside. You have never seen such a "defiant" chest.

t_ch: 踹一踹它。
t_en: Give it a kick.

t_ch: 停，别再碰了——我这就变回来。
t_en: Stop, don't hit me anymore... I will transform back.

t_ch: 宝箱变成了…一个皮皮西人，他已经被你的「观察力」彻底打败。
t_en: The chest transformed into... a Pepeshi. He has been completely defeated by your "powers of perception."

t_ch: 奇怪了，我这么逼真的拟态你都能认出来啊？
t_en: Strange, you managed to see through such a realistic mimicry?

t_ch: 请叫我银河球棒神探。
t_en: You can call me the Galactic Baseballer Detective.

t_ch: 是吗？怪不得你这么厉害……
t_en: Really? No wonder you're so amazing at this...

t_ch: 哪有宝箱不放东西的？
t_en: When have you ever seen an empty chest?

t_ch: 唔，原来是这样…看来你是用智慧战胜了我。
t_en: Oh, I see... So, it was your wisdom that allowed you to defeat me.

t_ch: 我只是随手开个宝箱…
t_en: I just wanted to open the chest...

t_ch: 唉，我知道你是想安慰我…我承认技不如你。
t_en: Ah, I know you're just trying to comfort me... I admit that I am no match for you.

t_ch: 其实还挺逼真的。
t_en: It really was quite realistic.

t_ch: 是吗？嘿嘿，看来我也挺厉害的……
t_en: Really? Hehe, I guess I am pretty amazing...

t_ch: 说起来，还是第一次有人愿意陪我这么玩。难道这就是传说中的…好朋友？！
t_en: Come to think of it, you're the first person that was ever willing to play with me! Does this mean that we're... best friends!?

t_ch: 之后有空的话，再来找杰诺一起玩吧！
t_en: If you get time, you have to come and play with me again!

t_ch: 奇怪的物品变回了皮皮西。
t_en: The strange object transformed back into a Pepeshi.

t_ch: 你又找到我了！我都快习惯了。
t_en: You found me again! I've come to expect this now...

t_ch: 不过下次我肯定会藏得更好，等着瞧吧！
t_en: I'll hide even better next time though, you'll see!

t_ch: 啧啧，这场面…也多亏智械皮糙肉厚，要是换成一般人啊，已经无了。
t_en: Tsk tsk, what a sight... Thank goodness Intellitrons are thick-skinned and meaty. They'd be a goner if it were a normal person.

t_ch: 智械压根不是有机生命，怎么能用皮糙肉厚来形容呢——应该是钢筋铁骨才对。
t_en: Intellitrons are not organic life at all. How can you call them tough-skinned and meaty — they're made of steel.

t_ch: 这车看起来还挺贵的，不知里面坐的人怎么样了……
t_en: That car looks quite expensive. I wonder what happened to the people in it...

t_ch: 哈，要是开车的家伙自己反倒被撞醒了，估计要在入梦池里气个半死吧。
t_en: Ha, if the person driving somehow got knocked awake, they'd probably be steaming mad in the Dreampool.

t_ch: 挺搞笑的，但是在匹诺康尼，倒也正常。
t_en: That's hilarious — but in Penacony, it's totally normal.

t_ch: 这一加长车，差点没给我外壳撞开胶了。
t_en: This limo nearly broke the glue seal on my outer shell.

t_ch: 你还好吧？
t_en: Are you all good?

t_ch: 好什么好，我这外壳都是手工抛光的，这一撞全给刮花了。
t_en: Nothing good about it. This shell of mine is hand-polished, but now it's all dinged up.

t_ch: 这里发生了什么？
t_en: What happened here?

t_ch: 看不出来吗？我被车撞了！可怜了我这一身的手工外壳，全给刮花了。
t_en: Can't you tell? I got hit by a car! My poor hand-polished shell... It's all scratched up.

t_ch: 本来我正常在路上走着， 那辆车不知发什么疯，直接穿过半个马路过来撞我——不知道的还以为车主歧视硅基生命呢。
t_en: I was just walking along the street as per normal when that car suddenly drove halfway across the lane to hit me —Who knows whether this was a hate crime targeting silicon-based lifeforms!

t_ch: 他要是不能给出满意的赔偿，这事我就跟他没完！
t_en: If he can't make a satisfactory settlement, then he'll never hear the end of this from me!

t_ch: 会不会太苛刻了点？
t_en: Wouldn't that be too harsh?

t_ch: 苛刻什么啊？能在匹诺康尼开这种车的都非富即贵，钱对他们来说就是数字而已。
t_en: What's so harsh about that? If you can afford to drive that kind of car in Penacony, then money is just a number to you.

t_ch: 说得对，得让他赔偿才行。
t_en: You're right. He should pay you compensation.

t_ch: 看来你脑子挺够用的。能在匹诺康尼开这种车的都非富即贵，钱对他们来说就是数字而已。
t_en: Looks like you've got a good head on your shoulders. If you can afford to drive that kind of car in Penacony, then money is just a number to you.

t_ch: （保持沉默）
t_en: (Remain silent)

t_ch: 我太了解这帮家伙了。能在匹诺康尼开这种车的都非富即贵，钱对他们来说就是数字而已。
t_en: I know these guys all too well. If you can afford to drive that kind of car in Penacony, then money is just a number to you.

t_ch: 既然他们能把钱从外面带到梦境里来，就别怪我把钱从他们口袋里带到外面去。
t_en: Since they can bring their money from outside into the Dreamscape, then don't blame me for taking their money from their pockets back to the outside.

t_ch: 这辆刚刚撞过人——或者被人撞过的车子停在原地，里面听不见半点声音。
t_en: The car that just hit someone — or got hit by someone — is stopped in place. No sound can be heard within.

t_ch: 敲敲车窗。
t_en: Knock on the window.

t_ch: 谁、谁啊？我不是说在家族的人来之前别打扰我吗！
t_en: Wh—who's that? Didn't I tell you not to bug me until The Family gets here!?

t_ch: 我是来了解情况的。
t_en: I'm here to sort out the situation.

t_ch: 那不打扰你了。
t_en: Then I won't bug you.

t_ch: 哪还有什么情况可了解？事实不是已经很清楚了吗——
t_en: What's there to sort out? Aren't the facts already clear enough...

t_ch: …我在路上开得好好的，那铁皮脑袋突然从一旁窜出来，直接往我的车上撞。
t_en: ...I was just driving on the street when that iron skull suddenly jumped out from the side and rammed straight into my car.

t_ch: 随着砰一声巨响，我的车被弹出去好几米，像是怼在了一块石头上似的。真离谱！
t_en: My car got sent off-course by several meters with a loud bang, as if I was hit by a rock. It's outrageous!

t_ch: 这年头碰瓷的手法都这么粗暴吗？太嚣张了，我在苜蓿草家系可是有熟人的！
t_en: Is this how they run insurance scams these days? So blatant! I'll have you know that I have contacts in the Alfalfa Family!

t_ch: 撞了人要给赔偿的吧？
t_en: Shouldn't you pay compensation after hitting someone with a car?

t_ch: 还赔偿？你看是他身上的坑大还是我车上的坑大？我才是受害者好不好！
t_en: Compensation? Is the dent on my car is bigger, or is the dent on him bigger? I'm the victim here, okay?
